[[Kevin Henderson (rugby league)|Kevin Henderson]] (brother) }} }} '''Andrew Henderson''' (born 17 June 1979) is a professional [[rugby league]] coach who is the Director of Rugby at [[Leigh Leopards]] in the [[English Super League]] and a former professional rugby league footballer who played for [[Scotland national rugby league team|Scotland]] at international level. He is the former assistant coach of the [[Warrington Wolves]] in the [[Super League]], the former head of rugby at the [[Keighley Cougars]] and former head coach of the [[London Broncos]]. He played professionally as a {{rlp|hk}}, representing [[Newcastle Thunder|Gateshead Thunder]], [[Castleford Tigers]], [[Balmain Tigers]], [[Salford Red Devils|Salford City Reds]], [[Barrow Raiders]] and the [[Sheffield Eagles]] over a 14 year playing career. He was also a former [[Scotland national rugby league team|Scotland]] international [[rugby league]] footballer. ==Background== He was born in [[Torquay]], [[Devon]], England. In 1988, his family emigrated to Australia.{{cite web|url=http://therfl.co.uk/news/article/13066 |title=Hendo teams up with Northern |publisher=Rugby Football League |date=13 February 2009 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101222011541/http://therfl.co.uk/news/article/13066 |archive-date=22 December 2010}} ==Playing career== Henderson began his professional career in England in 2001 with Super League side [[Salford Red Devils|Salford City Reds]]. With limited opportunities there, Henderson moved to Northern Ford Premiership club [[Barrow Raiders]] for the 2002 and 2003 seasons. He captained the Raiders for the 2003 season. Soon after, Super League side [[Widnes Vikings]] snapped up Henderson for a trial period, in which he featured for them in the 2004 Sydney World Sevens tournament, and a friendly match against the [[Wests Tigers]]. He failed to agree a deal with Widnes and subsequently joined the Wests Tigers, captaining their premier league side [[Balmain Tigers]] in 2004.{{citation needed|date=March 2023}} Henderson joined [[Castleford Tigers]] for the 2005 season after their relegation from the Super League.{{cn|date=November 2024}} He spent 4 seasons at the club. Henderson captained the side twice to promotion to the Super League in 2005 and 2007. The 2008 Super League season was to be his final year at the Tigers.{{citation needed|date=March 2023}} [[File:Andrew Henderson.JPG|thumb|left|Henderson playing for Gateshead Thunder]] Henderson signed for Championship side [[Newcastle Thunder|Gateshead Thunder]] for the 2009 season. He spent only one season with Gateshead as the club were faced with financial problems and a shareholders dispute leading them into administration. This forced him to look elsewhere and he joined Championship winners [[Barrow Raiders]] in the 2009 close season on a two-year deal. Henderson left Barrow not long after and joined [[Sheffield Eagles]] towards the end of the 2010 season, due to the Barrow club failing to honour his contract.{{cn|date=November 2024}} Henderson went on to play for just over four seasons with Sheffield, with 2014 being his final year. His time here was successful, helping his side make a grand final appearance in 2011 and achieve back to back grand final victories in 2012, and 2013. The Hendersons are a Scottish family and all three brothers played for [[Scotland national rugby league team|Scotland]] at international level. Henderson began his international career in 2003 featuring in the European Nations Cup. He went on to make 17 test appearances in European competitions and 5 World Cup appearances in 2008 and 2013 finishing his international career in Scotland's World Cup quarter final match against New Zealand in Leeds.{{cite news|work=BBC Sport|title=Scotland call up Super League six|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/international_and_australian/4282586.stm|access-date=2007-12-08|date=2005-09-26}}{{cite web|url=http://www.sportinglife.com/rugbyleague/news/story_get.cgi?STORY_NAME=rleague/08/05/29/RUGBYL_Scotland.html |title=Robertson ready for Scotland |publisher=Sportinglife |date=2008-05-30 |access-date=2008-05-30 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110605011136/http://www.sportinglife.com/rugbyleague/news/story_get.cgi?STORY_NAME=rleague%2F08%2F05%2F29%2FRUGBYL_Scotland.html |archive-date=2011-06-05 }}{{cite web|url=http://www.foxsports.com.au/story/0,8659,23782306-23214,00.html|archive-url=https://archive.today/20121206035930/http://www.foxsports.com.au/story/0,8659,23782306-23214,00.html|url-status=dead|archive-date=2012-12-06|title=Robertson set for kilt call-up|work=Fox Sports|date=2008-05-30|access-date=2008-05-30}} All three brothers featured in the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup squads#Scotland|Scotland squad]] for the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup]].{{cite web|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/7587971.stm|title=Henderson trio in Scots cup squad|publisher=BBC|date=2008-08-29|access-date=2008-10-03}} ==Coaching career== ===Sheffield Hallam Eagles=== On 24 February 2013, Henderson was appointed the head coach of a new community club, Sheffield Hallam Eagles, run by [[Sheffield Eagles]], and Sheffield Hallam University. In their first season, Henderson's team lost just once all season, winning the [[Conference League South]] beating [[Nottingham Outlaws (rugby league team)|Nottingham Outlaws]] in the grand final.{{citation needed|date=March 2023}} ===London Broncos=== On 11 July 2014, [[London Broncos]] head coach [[Joey Grima|Joe Grima]] appointed Henderson as his assistant beginning in November. On 2 March 2015, Joey Grima resigned after ten months in charge and just three games into the Championship season.{{Cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/31695108|title = Grima quits as Broncos head coach|work = BBC Sport}} Henderson took charge of the Championship side on an interim basis. After 2 games in charge with 1 win and 1 loss, he was appointed as the Broncos new permanent head coach.{{Cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/32075085|title = Henderson named Broncos head coach|work = BBC Sport}} In 2015, Henderson led the Broncos to 3rd place in the [[Championship Shield]], eventually losing the Shield Final to [[Featherstone Rovers]] 34–6 at the [[Select Security Stadium]] in Widnes. In 2016, he guided the London Broncos to a 2nd-place finish in the Championship taking them into the Super 8's Qualifiers for the chance to gain promotion. The club finished a reasonable 6th place on points difference with wins over Batley, Salford and Featherstone. They just missed out on the million pound game to Salford. In 2017, they again finished 2nd in the Championship and 6th place in the Super 8's Qualifiers. Henderson was awarded the Kingstone Press Championship Coach of the Year award after another successful season with London. The London Broncos won on all three occasions under the guidance of Henderson at the [[Summer Bash]] event. They recorded a 42–6 win and 32–14 win over the Sheffield Eagles in 2015 and 2016. In 2017 they defeated Featherstone Rovers 42–16. ===Warrington Wolves=== Henderson joined Warrington ahead of the 2018 season as assistant coach to Steve Price. He spent 4 seasons at the club before moving into a Head of Rugby role with the Keighley Cougars. During his time the team finished as Challenge Cup and grand finalists in 2018, followed by a Challenge Cup final win in 2019. His final two seasons saw the wolves finish as semi finalists in both competitions.{{citation needed|date=March 2023}} ===York Knights=== On 18 October 2022, Henderson signed a five-year deal to become the new head coach of [[York Knights]].{{cite web|url=https://www.warringtonguardian.co.uk/sport/wolves/wolvesnews/23057357.andrew-henderson-appointed-york-knights-head-coach/|title=Andrew Henderson appointed York Knights head coach|website=www.warringtonguardian.co.uk |date=18 October 2022 }} On 5 June 2024, it was reported Henderson had taken the position of Head of Rugby at York and that the head coach role had been handed over to [[Mark Applegarth]].{{cite web|website=Total RL|date=5 Jun 2024|url=https://www.totalrl.com/ex-wakefield-trinity-boss-mark-applegarth-appointed-to-championship-head-coach-role/|title=Ex-Wakefield Trinity boss Mark Applegarth appointed to Championship head coach role}} ==Coaching statistics== {{updated|23 September 2017}} {|class="wikitable" style="text-align: center" |- !rowspan="2"|Team !rowspan="2"|From !rowspan="2"|To !colspan="40"|Record |-
